text:
Market today: Risk-off tone driven by Apple's 6% drop on memory/chip supply concerns — that's a direct headwind for your chip-heavy book. When Apple sneezes, semis catch a cold. Margin exposure means even modest sector-wide selling hits your P&L harder than it looks.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest concern — down 17.41% and already flagged SELL; sitting well above your $85 stop but momentum is against you, act before it drifts lower. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.8% with a TRIM signal — Apple's memory crunch news hits Broadcom directly, this one needs size reduction now. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; tiny move to your $150 stop, one bad session wipes it. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: How semis (SMH) react to the Apple news intraday — if SMH breaks below $600, your whole book gets uglier fast. Also watch AVGO for any analyst downgrades tied to Apple supply headlines.

Bottom line: Cut or trim PLTR and AVGO today — margin means you can't afford to wait for a bounce that may not come.
